---
title: "Project structure"
description: "Source code, target code (Viash Hub vs local), and how versions are released."
order: 10
---

## Source code {#sec-project-structure}

An OpenPipeline repository holds **only source code** under `src/` — nothing
built. Each component is a `config.vsh.yaml` (its metadata: inputs, outputs,
dependencies, container) plus a script; each workflow is a config plus a
`main.nf`.

`src/` is organized into Viash [namespaces](https://viash.io/guide/): each
subfolder is a namespace grouping related components, and each subfolder within
it is one component. For example `src/convert/from_bdrhap_to_h5mu` is the
`from_bdrhap_to_h5mu` component in the `convert` namespace. Workflows live under
`src/workflows/` and are organized into namespaces the same way. Everything in
`src/` is handled by the same `viash` and `viash ns` commands.

## Target code — Viash Hub vs. local building

**Target code** is the runnable form built from the source: each component
becomes a container plus a Nextflow module. Source is never run directly — it is
always built first. That build happens in two places:

- **On Viash Hub (the published packages).** [Viash Hub](https://viash-hub.com)
  builds each package from its source and publishes versioned releases to
  [packages.viash-hub.com](https://packages.viash-hub.com). These are the
  official builds the [reference](../reference/index.qmd) documents and that
  users run — nothing built is ever committed to the source repository.
- **Locally (for development).** While developing you build it yourself with
  `viash ns build`, which writes the target code into a `target/` folder in your
  working copy. A fresh clone has no `target/`; it appears only after a local
  build, is git-ignored, and is never committed. It's purely for running and
  testing your work (see [Running tests](running-tests.qmd)).

## Versioning and releases {#sec-versioning}

OpenPipeline uses [semantic versioning](https://semver.org/) — every release is
numbered `MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH`:

- **MAJOR** — breaking changes to the public interface.
- **MINOR** — new, backward-compatible functionality. This can be substantial
  (large new features), but it never breaks existing usage.
- **PATCH** — backward-compatible bug fixes, with no container updates (the
  component containers are not rebuilt).

Changes for each release are recorded in the CHANGELOG.

Contributions are merged into the **`main`** branch. When a version is released,
Viash Hub builds that version of the package and publishes it to
[packages.viash-hub.com](https://packages.viash-hub.com). Every released version
stays available there, so:

- a run pinned to a version (e.g. `nextflow run … -r 4.2.0`) always gets exactly
  that build, and
- the [reference](../reference/index.qmd) documents a specific published version.

**Next:** [Creating components →](creating-components.qmd)
